---
title: Python quick start
description: Build an ACT component in Python with componentize-py and the act-sdk decorators.
---

## Scaffold

```bash
copier copy gh:actcore/act-template-python my-component
cd my-component
just init
```

The template sets up `pyproject.toml` with `act-sdk>=0.1.1`, a `wit/` directory tracking [`act:core@0.3.0`](https://github.com/actcore/act-spec/blob/main/wit/act-core.wit), `componentize-py` as a dev dependency, and a `justfile` with `build` / `test` / `publish`.

## Write a tool

`app.py`:

```python
from act_sdk import component, tool, ActError


@component
class MyComponent:
    @tool(description="Reverse a string", read_only=True)
    async def reverse(self, text: str) -> str:
        return text[::-1]

    @tool(description="Divide two numbers")
    async def divide(self, a: float, b: float) -> float:
        if b == 0:
            raise ActError("std:invalid-args", "Cannot divide by zero")
        return a / b
```

Type hints and the method signature become the tool's JSON Schema automatically. The `@component` decorator emits the WIT `tool-provider` export binding expected by `componentize-py`.

## Build

```bash
just build
```

This runs:
1. `uv run componentize-py -d wit -w component-world componentize app -o <name>.wasm`
2. `act-build pack <name>.wasm`

Python code is baked into a SpiderMonkey-free Python runtime via [`componentize-py`](https://github.com/bytecodealliance/componentize-py).

## Verify

```bash
act info <name>.wasm --tools
```

## Run

```bash
just test
act call <name>.wasm reverse --args '{"text":"hello"}'
```

## What `act_sdk` gives you

| Decorator / type | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `@component` | Marks a class as the ACT component, generates the WIT bridge |
| `@tool(description=..., read_only=..., idempotent=..., destructive=..., timeout_ms=...)` | Marks a method as a tool; JSON Schema derived from type hints |
| `ActError("std:kind", "msg")` | Raise for structured tool errors |
| `Json[T]` / `Content` | Opt into JSON or explicit MIME type (default is CBOR) |

Return types:

- `str` → text/plain
- `bytes` → application/octet-stream
- `Json(x)` → application/json
- `Content(mime, data)` → whatever you say
- anything else → CBOR

## Known limitations

- `componentize-py` currently requires Python 3.14 runtime to build.
- Cold-start is slower than Rust components (Python interpreter bundled).
